title: FURTHER STUDIES ON THE HYDROLYSIS OF STARCH  BY SWEET POTATO AMYLASE.
author: K. V. GIRI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The nature of the hydrolysis of amyloamylose and soluble starch by sweet potato amylase and β-amylase of barley is investigated. The sweet potato amylase behaves like a pure β-amylase. The amylase selectively hydrolyses a portion of the starch substance, leaving a residual material which resembles erythrogranulose. Amyloamylose is hydro­lysed completely by both the enzymes, without noteworthy inhibition, and the colour with iodine remains unchanged until almost complete saccharification takes place. During the saccharification of amyloarhylose by sweet potato amylase the intensity of the blue colour with iodine diminishes. The residual material is hydrolysed by α-amylase from barley, but it remains unaffected by sweet potato amylase.
steps_raw: The nature of the hydrolysis of amyloamylose and soluble starch by sweet potato amylase and β-amylase of barley is investigated. The sweet potato amylase behaves like a pure β-amylase. The amylase selectively hydrolyses a portion of the starch substance, leaving a residual material which resembles erythrogranulose. Amyloamylose is hydro­lysed completely by both the enzymes, without noteworthy inhibition, and the colour with iodine remains unchanged until almost complete saccharification takes place. During the saccharification of amyloarhylose by sweet potato amylase the intensity of the blue colour with iodine diminishes. The residual material is hydrolysed by α-amylase from barley, but it remains unaffected by sweet potato amylase.
